clusters.synthesis: Structured human review that evaluates meaning and intent instead of treating approvals as a mechanical click-through.
Optimizing agent work for user value rather than raw token output or surface-level throughput.
The shift from hardware-first thinking toward software-driven gains in AI performance.
Breaking implementation into coordinated vertical slices that deliver complete system behavior.
Using static analysis, exploit replays, UI checks, and regression gates to validate agent behavior before merge or release.
Managing the tradeoff between strict enforcement and false-positive noise that disrupts developer flow.
Agents that run for extended periods in the background with limited human steering and require durable orchestration.
Interfaces where agents initiate alerts or actions from shared context instead of only responding to direct prompts.
Scaling compute, data-center capacity, and chips to support larger parallel training efforts.
Containing agents and their tool calls in stronger sandbox boundaries such as micro-VMs for unsafe or production-capable execution.
Improving agents by generating candidates, evaluating them, and deploying the best-performing behaviors.
The stage where attention shifts from agents themselves to the harnesses and scaffolding around them.
The economics of buying, reselling, and negotiating model tokens under margin pressure and vendor leverage.
Collaborative documents that serve as live shared workspaces for humans and agents.
Attacks that steer model behavior by manipulating prompts or training data.
Trace and renderer machinery that preserves logical structure across tokenization and chat templates.
Chaining outline, search, and thematic shapes to answer mixed structured and unstructured questions.
High-quality qualitative judgment when objective metrics are missing or incomplete.
Rising model capability shifts where review happens and where proof must live.
Preserving operator understanding of the system, not just validating outputs after the fact.
Skills that fill current model gaps but may become unnecessary as base capability improves.
Using interaction traces to accumulate shared context and improve future agent behavior.
Useful capability that appears when models are paired with tools rather than only scaled in size or context.
Permission and logic failures that create first-foothold vulnerabilities in agent systems.
Measuring whether an agent's ideas spread through the community and get reused.
Preparing company facts, capabilities, and personas as reusable reference material before generation.
Projecting a source classification onto descendant nodes and edges after ingestion.

clusters.canonicalTopic: shared enterprise substrate for thin agents
clusters.synthesis: Building thinner agents that rely on a shared enterprise knowledge substrate instead of carrying all context themselves.
Using explicit ontology rules to constrain probabilistic agent behavior.
A queryable graph linking moments, entities, relationships, and corpus context for video understanding.
Using browser rendering to produce repeatable video output.
Improving evaluation quality from product feedback and textual signals rather than only hand-built metrics.
Graph structures that represent both additive DAGs and hierarchical rollups for organizations.
Task-specific models that replace the assumption that one model must handle everything.
Improving local model performance through tuning, quantization, and configuration on existing hardware.
Enterprise assistants maintaining durable understanding of a user and their work over time.
Using graph structure to answer questions that require multiple relationship hops.

clusters.canonicalTopic: breadth versus depth in software strategy
clusters.synthesis: Contrasting breadth and depth as a framing for modern product strategy.
Bundling schemas, tools, behaviors, and policies into reusable agent units.
